doc_comments.comment: Doc block comment: /-- ... --/  Must come before block_comments — both start with /-
doc_comments.name: comment.block.documentation.luc

patterns.comment: Raw string literal: r"..." — no escape processing, backslashes are literal
patterns.name: string.quoted.other.raw.luc

patterns.comment: Regular string literal: "..." with escape sequences
patterns.name: string.quoted.double.luc

patterns.comment: Unicode escape: \UXXXXXXXX (8 hex digits, full codepoint)
patterns.name: constant.character.escape.unicode.long.luc

patterns.comment: Unicode escape: \uXXXX (4 hex digits, BMP codepoint)
patterns.name: constant.character.escape.unicode.luc

patterns.comment: Hex byte escape: \xHH
patterns.name: constant.character.escape.hex.luc

patterns.comment: Standard single-char escapes: \n \t \r \\ \" \' \0
patterns.name: constant.character.escape.luc

patterns.comment: Unrecognised escape — surfaces as an error
patterns.name: invalid.illegal.escape.luc

patterns.comment: Hex literal: 0xFF  0xDEAD_BEEF
patterns.name: constant.numeric.hex.luc

patterns.comment: Binary literal: 0b1010  0b1111_0000
patterns.name: constant.numeric.binary.luc

patterns.comment: Float with optional exponent: 3.14  1.5e-3  1_000.0
patterns.name: constant.numeric.float.luc

patterns.comment: Float exponent without decimal point: 1e10
patterns.name: constant.numeric.float.luc

patterns.comment: Integer literal: 42  1_000_000
patterns.name: constant.numeric.integer.luc

keywords_declaration.comment: Variable / function declaration keywords: let imt val
keywords_declaration.name: keyword.declaration.luc

keywords_module.comment: Module-system keywords: package module use as
keywords_module.name: keyword.other.module.luc

keywords_logical.comment: Word-form logical operators: and or not
keywords_logical.name: keyword.operator.logical.luc

type_decl_keywords.comment: Type-declaration keywords: struct enum trait type impl from
type_decl_keywords.name: keyword.declaration.type.luc

patterns.comment: Composition operator +> — compile-time function wiring; must be matched before plain +
patterns.name: keyword.operator.compose.luc

patterns.comment: Pipeline operator -> — runtime left-to-right execution; must be matched before plain -
patterns.name: keyword.operator.pipeline.luc

patterns.comment: Nil-coalescing fallback: ??  (must be matched before single ?)
patterns.name: keyword.operator.nullcoalesce.luc

patterns.comment: Nullable chain: .?  (must be matched before plain . and plain ?)
patterns.name: keyword.operator.nullchain.luc

patterns.comment: Nullable type suffix: ?
patterns.name: keyword.operator.nullable.luc

operators_compound_assign.comment: Compound assignment operators: += -= *= /= ^= %=  (must be matched before plain = and arithmetic)
operators_compound_assign.name: keyword.operator.assignment.compound.luc

operators_comparison.comment: Comparison / equality: == != <= >= < >  (must be matched before plain = and < >)
operators_comparison.name: keyword.operator.comparison.luc

patterns.comment: Bit XOR: ~^  (must be matched before plain ~)
patterns.name: keyword.operator.bitwise.xor.luc

patterns.comment: Left shift: <<  (must be matched before plain <)
patterns.name: keyword.operator.bitwise.shift.left.luc

patterns.comment: Right shift: >>  (must be matched before plain >)
patterns.name: keyword.operator.bitwise.shift.right.luc

patterns.comment: Bitwise NOT / complement: ~
patterns.name: keyword.operator.bitwise.not.luc

operators_arithmetic.comment: Arithmetic operators: + - * / % ^  (plain forms, after compound/pipeline/bitwise already matched)
operators_arithmetic.name: keyword.operator.arithmetic.luc

patterns.comment: Variadic parameter marker: ...  (must be matched before ..)
patterns.name: keyword.operator.variadic.luc

patterns.comment: Range literal: ..  (must be matched before plain .)
patterns.name: keyword.operator.range.luc

patterns.comment: Raw pointer sigil / unsafe bit-reinterpret prefix: @
patterns.name: keyword.operator.pointer.luc

patterns.comment: Safe reference type prefix: &
patterns.name: keyword.operator.reference.luc

patterns.comment: Union type separator (also bitwise OR in expression context): |
patterns.name: keyword.operator.union.luc

patterns.comment: Argument-pack annotation on a pipeline step: fn(args)!
patterns.name: keyword.operator.pack.luc

patterns.comment: Plain assignment: =  (after compound assignments already matched)
patterns.name: keyword.operator.assignment.luc

patterns.comment: Wildcard pattern in match: _  (standalone, not part of an identifier)
patterns.name: variable.language.wildcard.luc

patterns.comment: Behavior-member / impl-namespace access, generic constraint, match field pattern: :
patterns.name: punctuation.accessor.impl.luc

patterns.comment: Data-member access and module-path separator: .  (after .? and .. already matched)
patterns.name: punctuation.accessor.dot.luc

patterns.comment: Comma — optional separator throughout the language
patterns.name: punctuation.separator.comma.luc

patterns.comment: Semicolon — optional separator throughout the language
patterns.name: punctuation.separator.semicolon.luc

patterns.comment: Block delimiters: { }
patterns.name: punctuation.definition.block.luc

patterns.comment: Group / parameter-list delimiters: ( )
patterns.name: punctuation.definition.group.luc

patterns.comment: Array / index delimiters: [ ]
patterns.name: punctuation.definition.array.luc
